# meatywiki-portal — Knowledge State

The MeatyWiki Portal is a Next.js 15 web frontend for the MeatyWiki knowledge compilation engine, serving as a sibling to the Python backend monorepo [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV]. It allows users to browse artifacts, capture notes/URLs, monitor workflow runs, and initiate compilations without using the CLI [source: art_01KTCNK5WCRFMCANDFKDMC15TQ]. Key screens include Inbox, Library, Artifact Detail (with Source/Knowledge/Draft readers), Quick Add, and Workflow Status surface [source: art_01KTCNK5WCRFMCANDFKDMC15TQ]. The tech stack uses Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, Jest + React Testing Library, and Playwright for E2E testing [source: art_01KTCNK5WCRFMCANDFKDMC15TQ]. Core invariants include: write-through-engine (all vault mutations go through backend API, never directly from frontend) [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV], no backend imports (HTTP only) [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV], local-only default at http://127.0.0.1:8787 [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV], and bearer token stored in HttpOnly cookie only, never localStorage [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV]. Portal v1.5 added an Intellectual Archive editorial reskin, status-driven Inbox triage, App Home landing page, contextual Workflows Dashboard metrics, and experimental PWA support [source: art_01KTCNK5WCRFMCANDFKDMC15TQ], plus a Lens Badge write path via PATCH /api/artifacts/:id/lens and an IndexedDB offline queue [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV]. Pipeline observability added in v1.7+ includes SSE terminal events (compile_failed, stage_degraded), a Processing History tab on Artifact Detail, and degradation/failure indicators [source: art_01KTCNK5WEDSRWDNXWJDS90EKV].
